title: A song I sing without thinking
summary: Describe a song you find yourself singing during the day without trying.
topic: A song I sing without thinking
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a song you sing without thinking. Say where you usually sing it, how you learned it, and how you feel when you sing it. Use present simple.
hintStarters: When I cook dinner in my flat in Mexico City, I…
I do not choose this song; it just…
My mother used to sing it because…
sampleText: When I cook dinner in my flat in Mexico City, I sing one song again and again. I do not choose this song. It just comes out of my mouth. The song is a slow old song in Spanish. It is about a man who walks home after a long day. The tune is very simple — only four or five notes. I do not even remember all the words. I sing the chorus and I hum the rest. My mother sang this song when she was tired. She washed the dishes and she sang in a soft voice. I was six years old. I sat on a small chair near her. I did not know what the words meant. Now I cook dinner alone, and the same song comes back. I sing it when I cut onions. I sing it in the shower. I sing it on the bus when nobody can hear. The song is not famous. It is not on the radio. But it lives inside me. When I sing it, I feel safe and a little happy.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a slow old song in Spanish
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một bài hát cũ chậm bằng tiếng Tây Ban Nha

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I sing the chorus and I hum the rest
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi hát điệp khúc và ngân nga phần còn lại

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I sat on a small chair near her
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi ngồi trên chiếc ghế nhỏ bên cạnh mẹ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: on the bus when nobody can hear
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trên xe buýt khi không ai nghe

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time clauses with 'when'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'when' nối hai sự việc xảy ra cùng lúc: 'When I cook dinner', 'When I sing it', 'when nobody can hear'. Sau 'when' là một mệnh đề đầy đủ (chủ ngữ + động từ).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'when' joins two events: 'When I cook dinner', 'When I sing it', 'when nobody can hear'. After 'when' you need a full clause (subject + verb).

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Object pronoun 'it'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi tránh lặp danh từ 'the song', dùng đại từ 'it': 'I sing it when I cut onions'. 'it' đứng sau động từ, làm tân ngữ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: To avoid repeating 'the song', use the pronoun 'it': 'I sing it when I cut onions'. 'it' goes after the verb as the object.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi nấu bữa tối trong căn hộ ở Mexico City, tôi hát một bài hát đi đi lại lại. Tôi không chọn bài này. Nó tự bật ra khỏi miệng tôi. Bài hát là một bài cũ chậm bằng tiếng Tây Ban Nha. Bài nói về một người đàn ông đi bộ về nhà sau một ngày dài. Giai điệu rất đơn giản — chỉ bốn hoặc năm nốt. Tôi thậm chí không nhớ hết lời. Tôi hát điệp khúc và ngân nga phần còn lại. Mẹ tôi đã hát bài này khi mẹ mệt. Mẹ rửa bát và hát bằng giọng nhẹ. Tôi sáu tuổi. Tôi ngồi trên một cái ghế nhỏ gần mẹ. Tôi không biết lời bài hát có nghĩa gì. Bây giờ tôi nấu bữa tối một mình, và bài hát đó quay trở lại. Tôi hát khi cắt hành. Tôi hát trong phòng tắm. Tôi hát trên xe buýt khi không ai nghe. Bài hát không nổi tiếng. Bài không có trên radio. Nhưng bài hát sống trong tôi. Khi hát bài đó, tôi cảm thấy an toàn và hơi vui.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where does the writer sing the song?
options: When cooking, in the shower, and on the bus
mcQuestions.explanation: The text gives three places: while cutting onions, in the shower, and on the bus.
mcQuestions.hint: The writer lists more than one place.

mcQuestions.prompt: What language is the song in?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'a slow old song in Spanish'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who used to sing this song?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'My mother sang this song when she was tired'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How old was the writer in the memory?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I was six years old'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer feel when he sings the song?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I feel safe and a little happy'.

options: When I cook dinner, I sing one song.
When cook dinner, I sing one song.
When I cook dinner I sing when.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'when', use subject + verb: 'When I cook dinner'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'when' you need a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct pronoun: 'I love this song. I sing ___ every day.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'song' is a thing, so use the object pronoun 'it'.
mcQuestions.hint: A song is not a person.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is in past simple?
What does the writer say about the song's popularity?
options: It is very famous and on the radio.
It is not famous and not on the radio.
Everyone in the city sings it.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The song is not famous. It is not on the radio'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the two short sentences near the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple to describe a current habit?
options: I sang the song last night.
I sing the song when I cook dinner.
I will sing the song tomorrow.
I am singing the song now.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'when I cook dinner' shows a habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the regular activity form.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do songs from childhood often stay with us much longer than songs we hear as adults?
